```
25: // Find selected phrase and calculate its visual row index
26:   type PhraseWithSync = { id: string; name: string; startPosition: number; duration: number; originalStartPosition?: number; headLength: number; anchoredFrame?: number; originalAnchoredFrame?: number };
27:   let selectedPhrase: PhraseWithSync | null = null;
28:   let selectedTrack: { id: string; phrases: PhraseWithSync[] } | null = null;
29:   let trackIndex = -1;
30: 
31:   const trackList = tracks || [];
32:   for (let i = 0; i < trackList.length; i++) {
33:     const t = trackList[i];
34:     for (const p of (t.phrases || [])) {
35:       if (p.id === selectedPhraseId) {
36:         selectedPhrase = p as PhraseWithSync;
37:         selectedTrack = t as { id: string; phrases: PhraseWithSync[] };
38:         trackIndex = i;
39:         break;
40:       }
41:     }
42:     if (selectedPhrase) break;
43:   }
44:   
45:   useEffect(() => {
46:     const handleClickOutside = (e: MouseEvent) => {
47:       if (toolRef.current && !toolRef.current.contains(e.target as Node)) {
48:         // Also check if we clicked on a wavesurfer element to avoid immediately closing when selecting a new phrase
49:         const target = e.target as HTMLElement;
50:         if (!target.closest('wave') && !target.closest('.multitrack-container')) {
51:           setSelectedPhraseId(null);
52:         }
53:       }
54:     };
55:     document.addEventListener('mousedown', handleClickOutside);
56:     return () => document.removeEventListener('mousedown', handleClickOutside);
57:   }, [setSelectedPhraseId]);
58:   
59:   useEffect(() => {
60:     let frameId: number;
61:     const updateScroll = () => {
62:       const container = document.querySelector('.multitrack-container');
63:       if (container && container.firstChild instanceof HTMLElement && container.firstChild.shadowRoot) {
64:         const scrollContainer = container.firstChild.shadowRoot.querySelector('.scroll');
65:         if (scrollContainer) {
66:           setScrollLeft(scrollContainer.scrollLeft);
67:           setScrollTop(scrollContainer.scrollTop);
68:         }
69:       }
70:       frameId = requestAnimationFrame(updateScroll);
71:     };
72:     frameId = requestAnimationFrame(updateScroll);
73:     return () => cancelAnimationFrame(frameId);
74:   }, []);
75:   
76:   if (!selectedPhrase || trackIndex === -1) return null;
77:   
78:   const rawLeft = (selectedPhrase.startPosition * zoom) - scrollLeft;
79:   // Estimate tool width to keep it on screen
80:   const toolWidth = 380;
81:   const left = Math.max(10, Math.min(rawLeft, window.innerWidth - toolWidth - 20));
82:   
83:   // Calculate actual top position by summing heights of preceding tracks
84:   let topOffset = 0;
85:   for (let i = 0; i < trackIndex; i++) {
86:     const t = trackList[i];
87:     const isMetronome = t.id === 'metronome';
88:     const isExpanded = t.id === selectedTrackId && !envelopeLocked && !isMetronome;
89:     topOffset += isMetronome ? 40 : (isExpanded ? 80 : 50);
90:   }
91:   
92:   // Position it just below the track
93:   const selTrack = trackList[trackIndex];
94:   const isSelMetronome = selTrack.id === 'metronome';
95:   const isSelExpanded = selTrack.id === selectedTrackId && !envelopeLocked && !isSelMetronome;
96:   const selHeight = isSelMetronome ? 40 : (isSelExpanded ? 80 : 50);
97:   
98:   // Account for the timeline height (approx 20px)
99:   const timelineHeight = 20;
100:   const top = topOffset + selHeight + timelineHeight - scrollTop;
101:   
102:   const handleOffset = (amount: number) => {
103:     if (!selectedPhrase || !selectedTrack) return;
104:     const newPosition = selectedPhrase.startPosition + amount;
105:     if (newPosition < 0) {
106:       // If we try to move before 0, we instead move ALL OTHER phrases forward
107:       // by the amount we would have gone negative, and set this phrase to 0.
108:       const shiftAmount = Math.abs(newPosition);
109:       shiftAllPhrases(shiftAmount, selectedPhrase.id);
110:       updatePhrasePosition(selectedTrack.id, selectedPhrase.id, 0);
111:     } else {
112:       updatePhrasePosition(selectedTrack.id, selectedPhrase.id, newPosition);
113:     }
114:   };
115:   
116:   const toggleLoop = () => {
117:     if (!selectedPhrase) return;
118:     if (syncLoop) {
119:       setSyncLoop(null);
120:     } else {
121:       setSyncLoop({
122:         start: Math.max(0, selectedPhrase.startPosition - 2),
123:         end: selectedPhrase.startPosition + selectedPhrase.duration + 2
124:       });
125:     }
126:   };
127:   
128:   return (
129:     <div 
130:       ref={toolRef}
131:       className="absolute z-50 bg-gray-800 border border-gray-600 rounded-lg shadow-xl p-3 flex flex-col gap-3 transition-all duration-100 min-w-[360px]"
132:       style={{ left, top }}
133:     >
134:       {/* Row 1: Name and Head Length */}
135:       <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
136:         <div className="flex flex-col gap-1">
137:           <div className="text-[10px] text-gray-500 uppercase font-bold tracking-tighter">Phrase Name</div>
138:           <input 
139:             type="text"
140:             value={selectedPhrase.name || ''}
141:             onChange={(e) => updatePhrase(selectedTrack.id, selectedPhrase.id, { name: e.target.value })}
142:             onKeyDown={(e) => {
143:               if (e.key === 'Enter') {
144:                 e.currentTarget.blur();
145:               }
146:             }}
147:             className="bg-gray-900 border border-gray-700 rounded px-1.5 py-0.5 text-xs text-white focus:outline-none focus:border-indigo-500 w-24"
148:             placeholder="e.g. Take 1"
149:             onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}
150:           />
151:         </div>
152: 
153:         <div className="w-px h-8 bg-gray-600 mx-1"></div>
154: 
155:         <div className="flex flex-col gap-1">
156:           <div className="text-[10px] text-gray-500 uppercase font-bold tracking-tighter">Head (s)</div>
157:           <input 
158:             type="number"
159:             min="0"
160:             max="1"
161:             step="0.1"
162:             value={selectedPhrase.headLength}
163:             onChange={(e) => {
164:               const val = parseFloat(e.target.value);
165:               if (!isNaN(val)) {
166:                 updatePhrase(selectedTrack.id, selectedPhrase.id, { headLength: val });
167:               }
168:             }}
169:             onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}
170:             className="bg-gray-900 border border-gray-700 rounded px-1.5 py-0.5 text-xs text-white focus:outline-none focus:border-indigo-500 w-14 text-right"
171:           />
172:         </div>
173: 
174:         <div className="flex flex-col gap-1 flex-1">
175:           <div className="text-[10px] text-gray-300 font-medium mb-1">Nudge Clip Position:</div>
176:           <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
177:             <button onClick={() => handleOffset(-1)} className="px-2 py-1 bg-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-600 rounded text-[10px] text-white transition-colors" title="Shift earlier by 1 second">-1s</button>
178:             <button onClick={() => handleOffset(-0.1)} className="px-2 py-1 bg-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-600 rounded text-[10px] text-white transition-colors" title="Shift earlier by 100 milliseconds">-0.1s</button>
179:             <button onClick={() => handleOffset(-0.01)} className="px-2 py-1 bg-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-600 rounded text-[10px] text-white transition-colors" title="Shift earlier by 10 milliseconds">-10ms</button>
180:             <div className="w-px h-4 bg-gray-600 mx-1"></div>
181:             <button onClick={() => handleOffset(0.01)} className="px-2 py-1 bg-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-600 rounded text-[10px] text-white transition-colors" title="Shift later by 10 milliseconds">+10ms</button>
182:             <button onClick={() => handleOffset(0.1)} className="px-2 py-1 bg-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-600 rounded text-[10px] text-white transition-colors" title="Shift later by 100 milliseconds">+0.1s</button>
183:           </div>
184:         </div>
185:       </div>
186: 
187:       {/* Row 2: Actions */}
188:       <div className="flex items-center gap-2 pt-2 border-t border-gray-700">
189:         {selectedPhrase.originalAnchoredFrame !== undefined && selectedPhrase.originalAnchoredFrame !== 0 && (
190:           <>
191:             <button 
192:               onClick={() => {
193:                 // Reset: restore anchor and calculate new startPosition
194:                 // startPosition = anchor(RealTime) - secondsPerBar(UserTime offset) - headLength
195:                 const state = useStore.getState();
196:                 const anchorFrame = selectedPhrase.originalAnchoredFrame;
197:                 const sampleRate = state.sampleRate;
198:                 const beatsPerSecond = (state.bpm || 120) / 60;
199:                 const secondsPerBar = (1 / beatsPerSecond) * (state.timeSignature?.[0] || 4);
200:                 const newStartPosition = (anchorFrame / sampleRate) - secondsPerBar - selectedPhrase.headLength;
201:                 updatePhrase(selectedTrack.id, selectedPhrase.id, {
202:                   anchoredFrame: anchorFrame,
203:                   startPosition: Math.max(0, newStartPosition)
204:                 });
205:               }} 
206:               className="px-2 py-1 bg-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-600 rounded text-[10px] text-white transition-colors" 
207:               title="Reset to original recorded position"
208:             >
209:               Reset
210:             </button>
211:             
212:             {selectedPhrase.startPosition < selectedPhrase.originalStartPosition! && (
213:               <button 
214:                 onClick={() => {
215:                   const latencySec = selectedPhrase.originalStartPosition! - selectedPhrase.startPosition;
216:                   useStore.getState().setGlobalLatencyMs(Math.round(latencySec * 1000));
217:                   alert(`Global latency compensation set to ${Math.round(latencySec * 1000)}ms`);
218:                 }} 
219:                 className="px-2 py-1 bg-indigo-600 hover:bg-indigo-500 rounded text-[10px] text-white transition-colors" 
220:                 title="Set this offset as the global latency compensation for future recordings"
221:               >
222:                 Set Global
223:               </button>
224:             )}
225:             <div className="w-px h-4 bg-gray-600 mx-1"></div>
226:           </>
227:         )}
228:         
229:         <button 
230:           onClick={toggleLoop} 
231:           className={`px-2 py-1 rounded text-[10px] flex items-center transition-colors ${syncLoop ? 'bg-indigo-600 text-white' : 'bg-gray-700 text-gray-300 hover:bg-gray-600'}`}
232:           title="Loop playback ±2s around clip"
233:         >
234:           <Repeat className="w-3 h-3 mr-1" />
235:           Loop ±2s
236:         </button>
237:         
238:         <div className="flex-1"></div>
239:         
240:         <button 
241:           onClick={() => {
242:             removePhrase(selectedTrack.id, selectedPhrase.id);
243:             setSelectedPhraseId(null);
244:             if (syncLoop) setSyncLoop(null);
245:           }}

247:           title="Delete clip"

255:             setSyncLoop(null);
256:           }}

261:         </button>
```

```
122:   const muteLongPressTimer = useRef<{ [key: string]: NodeJS.Timeout | null }>({});
123: 
124:   // Initial seek to 00:00:00 (Bar 1)
125:   useEffect(() => {
126:     if (isReady) {
127:       seekTo(0);
128:     }
129:   }, [isReady, seekTo]);
130: 
131:   // Handle Responsive Layout
132:   useEffect(() => {
133:     // Force-reset persisted layout state that might cause issues
134:     useStore.setState({ 
135:       toolbarProposal: 1, 
136:       appMode: 'mixer',
137:       sidebarWidth: Math.floor(window.innerWidth * 0.25) 
138:     });
139:     
140:     const handleResize = () => {
141:       const headerHeight = window.innerWidth < 640 ? 48 : 64;
142:       const availableHeight = window.innerHeight - headerHeight - 4; // -4 for playhead slider height
143:       
144:       // We want to fit at least 6 items (5 tracks + metronome)
145:       // Let's say metronome is 0.8 of a normal track height
146:       const totalUnits = 5 + 0.8;
147:       let calculatedTrackHeight = Math.floor(availableHeight / totalUnits);
148:       
149:       // Constraints
150:       const maxTrackHeight = 150;
151:       const minTrackHeight = 40;
152:       
153:       if (calculatedTrackHeight > maxTrackHeight) calculatedTrackHeight = maxTrackHeight;
154:       if (calculatedTrackHeight < minTrackHeight) calculatedTrackHeight = minTrackHeight;
155:       
156:       const calculatedMetronomeHeight = Math.floor(calculatedTrackHeight * 0.8);
157:       const calculatedSidebarWidth = Math.floor(window.innerWidth * 0.25);
158:       
159:       // Hide labels if screen is too narrow (e.g., mobile portrait)
160:       const shouldShowLabels = window.innerWidth > 500;
161: 
162:       setResponsiveLayout({
163:         trackHeight: calculatedTrackHeight,
164:         metronomeHeight: calculatedMetronomeHeight,
165:         sidebarWidth: calculatedSidebarWidth
166:       });
167:       useStore.getState().setToolbarVisibleLabels(shouldShowLabels);
168:     };
169: 
170:     handleResize();
171:     window.addEventListener('resize', handleResize);
172:     return () => window.removeEventListener('resize', handleResize);
173:   }, [setResponsiveLayout]);
174: 
175:   // Close modals on Escape and handle Spacebar for Play/Pause
176:   useEffect(() => {
177:     const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
178:       if (e.key === 'Escape') {
179:         setShowSettings(false);
180:         setShowTracksModal(false);
181:         setShowMetronomeSettings(false);
182:         setEditingTrackId(null);
183:         setSelectedPhraseId(null);
184:        } else if (e.key === 'd') { // Reset toolbar and app mode
185:         useStore.getState().setToolbarProposal(1);
186:         useStore.getState().setToolbarVisibleLabels(true);
187:         useStore.getState().setAppMode('mixer');
188:       } else if (e.key === ' ' && !(e.target instanceof HTMLInputElement || e.target instanceof HTMLTextAreaElement)) {
189:         e.preventDefault();
190:         e.stopPropagation();
191:         playPause();
192:       }
193:     };
194:     window.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown, { capture: true });
195:     return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown, { capture: true });
196:   }, [setSelectedPhraseId, playPause, setShowTracksModal]);
197:   const lastTapTime = useRef<{ [key: string]: number }>({});
198: 
199: 
200: 
201:   // Sync Phrase Names and Selection to DOM for CSS labeling
202:   useEffect(() => {
203:     if (!isReady || !multitrackRef.current) return;
204:     
205:     const multitrack = multitrackRef.current;
206:     const wss = multitrack.wavesurfers || [];
207:     
208:     // Match wavesurfers to phrases based on the order they were added in useAudioEngine
209:     const allPhrases: { id: string; name: string }[] = [];
210:     
211:     (tracks || []).forEach(t => {
212:       if (t.phrases.length === 0) {
213:         allPhrases.push({ id: `empty_${t.id}`, name: '' });
214:       } else {
215:         t.phrases.forEach(p => allPhrases.push(p as { id: string; name: string }));
216:       }
217:     });
218: 
219:     wss.forEach((ws: { getWrapper: () => HTMLElement }, i: number) => {
220:       const phrase = allPhrases[i];
221:       if (!phrase) return;
222:       
223:       const wrapper = ws.getWrapper();
224:       if (wrapper) {
225:         // Add attributes for CSS labeling and selection highlighting
226:         if (phrase.id.startsWith('empty_')) {
227:           wrapper.removeAttribute('data-phrase-id');
228:           wrapper.removeAttribute('data-selected');
229:         } else {
230:           wrapper.setAttribute('data-phrase-id', phrase.id);
231:           wrapper.setAttribute('data-selected', (phrase.id === selectedPhraseId).toString());
232:         }
233:       }
234:     });
235:   }, [tracks, selectedPhraseId, isReady, multitrackRef]);
236:   
237:   // Track which tracks have already been synced to multitrack to avoid redundant addTrack calls
238:   const syncedTrackIdsRef = useRef<Set<string>>(new Set());
239: 
240:   // Update multitrack tracks when anchoredFrame changes (for playback sync)
241:   // FIXED: Only addTrack for new tracks or when anchoredFrame actually changes meaningfully
242:   // to avoid destroying and recreating audio nodes on every render
243:   useEffect(() => {
244:     if (!isReady || !multitrackRef.current) return;
245: 
246:     tracks.forEach(track => {
247:       if (track.anchoredFrame !== undefined && track.anchoredFrame !== 0) {
248:         // Check if this track needs updating
249:         const alreadySynced = syncedTrackIdsRef.current.has(track.id);
250: 
251:         if (!alreadySynced) {
252:           console.log('useEffect anchoredFrame: initial sync for track', track.id, 'anchoredFrame=', track.anchoredFrame);
253:           syncedTrackIdsRef.current.add(track.id);
254: 
255:           // Convert Track to TrackOptions (multitrack expects this)
256:           const isMetronome = track.id === 'metronome';
257:           const trackOffset = track.offset || 0;
258:           const beatsPerSecond = (bpm || 120) / 60;
259:           const secondsPerBeat = 1 / beatsPerSecond;
260:           const spb = secondsPerBeat * (timeSignature?.[0] || 4);
261:           const trackOptions = {
262:             id: track.id,
263:             name: track.name,
264:             color: track.color,
265:             isMuted: track.isMuted,
266:             isSolo: track.isSolo,
267:             volume: track.volume,
268:             pan: track.pan,
269:             offset: track.offset,
270:             anchoredFrame: track.anchoredFrame,
271:             phrases: track.phrases.map(p => ({
272:               url: p.url,
273:               audioBuffer: p.audioBuffer,
274:               peaks: p.peaks,
275:               // FIXED: Convert UserTime → RealTime for multitrack
276:               startPosition: isMetronome ? p.startPosition : p.startPosition + spb + trackOffset,
277:               duration: p.duration,
278:               headLength: p.headLength,
279:               anchoredFrame: p.anchoredFrame,
280:               originalAnchoredFrame: p.originalAnchoredFrame,
281:             })),
282:           } as any;
283:           multitrackRef.current?.addTrack(trackOptions);
284:         }
285:       }
286:     });
287:   }, [tracks, isReady, multitrackRef]);
288: 
289:   const handleContainerClick = (e: React.MouseEvent) => {
290:     if (!containerRef.current || !multitrackRef.current) return;
291:     
292:     // Disallow seeking during recording
293:     if (useStore.getState().isRecording) return;
294: 
295:     // Only handle if clicking on the background, not on a phrase
296:     if ((e.target as HTMLElement).closest('.phrase-container')) return;
297: 
298:     // Stop propagation to prevent MultiTrack's built-in click handler from firing
299:     e.stopPropagation();
300: 
301:     const rect = containerRef.current.getBoundingClientRect();
302:     const x = e.clientX - rect.left;
303:     
304:     const beatsPerSecond = (bpm || 120) / 60;
305:     const secondsPerBeat = 1 / beatsPerSecond;
306:     const secondsPerBar = secondsPerBeat * (timeSignature?.[0] || 4);
307: 
308:     // Get scroll position from the MultiTrack scroll container
309:     let scrollLeft = 0;
310:     const scrollContainer = containerRef.current.querySelector('.multitrack-scroll-container') as HTMLElement;
311:     if (scrollContainer) {
312:       scrollLeft = scrollContainer.scrollLeft;
313:     }
314: 
315:     const realTime = (x + scrollLeft) / zoom;
316:     const userTime = realTime - secondsPerBar;
317:     console.log('handleContainerClick', { x, scrollLeft, zoom, secondsPerBar, realTime, userTime });
318:     
319:     console.log('Click:', { x, scrollLeft, zoom, realTime, userTime, secondsPerBar });
320:     
321:     seekTo(userTime);
322:   };
323: 
324:   // Add native double-click listener to the container
325:   useEffect(() => {
326:     const container = containerRef.current;
327:     if (!container) return;
328: 
329:     const handleWheel = (e: WheelEvent) => {
330:       // If horizontal scroll, let it pan natively
331:       if (Math.abs(e.deltaX) > Math.abs(e.deltaY)) {
332:         return;
333:       }
334: 
335:       // Vertical scroll -> zoom
336:       e.preventDefault();
337: 
338:       const state = useStore.getState();
339:       const currentZoom = state.zoom;
340:       
341:       // Calculate zoom delta
342:       // e.deltaY > 0 means scrolling down (zoom out)
343:       // e.deltaY < 0 means scrolling up (zoom in)
344:       const zoomFactor = e.deltaY > 0 ? 0.9 : 1.1;
345:       let newZoom = currentZoom * zoomFactor;
346:       newZoom = Math.max(10, Math.min(200, newZoom)); // Clamp zoom
347: 
348:       if (Math.abs(newZoom - currentZoom) < 0.1) return;
349: 
350:       // Find scroll container
351:       let scrollContainer: HTMLElement | null = null;
352:       const firstChild = container.firstChild;
353:       if (firstChild instanceof HTMLElement && firstChild.shadowRoot) {
354:         scrollContainer = firstChild.shadowRoot.querySelector('.scroll');
355:       } else if (container.shadowRoot) {
356:         scrollContainer = container.shadowRoot.querySelector('.scroll');
357:       }
358: 
359:       if (scrollContainer) {
360:         const rect = scrollContainer.getBoundingClientRect();
361:         const pointerX = e.clientX - rect.left;
362:         const scrollLeft = scrollContainer.scrollLeft;
363:         
364:         // Time at pointer
365:         const pointerTime = (scrollLeft + pointerX) / currentZoom;
366: 
367:         // Apply new zoom directly to multitrack if available
368:         if (multitrackRef.current) {
369:           try {
370:             multitrackRef.current.zoom(newZoom);
371:             if (typeof multitrackRef.current.setOptions === 'function') {
372:               multitrackRef.current.setOptions({ minPxPerSec: newZoom });
373:             }
374:           } catch (e) {
375:             console.warn("Error zooming directly:", e);
376:           }
377:         }
378: 
379:         // Apply new zoom to state
380:         state.setZoom(newZoom);
381: 
382:         // Calculate new scroll position
383:         // Since we zoomed directly, we can set scrollLeft immediately
384:         scrollContainer.scrollLeft = pointerTime * newZoom - pointerX;
385:       } else {
386:         state.setZoom(newZoom);
387:       }
388:     };
389: 
390:     container.addEventListener('wheel', handleWheel, { passive: false });
391: 
392:     const handleDoubleClick = (e: MouseEvent) => {
393:       const state = useStore.getState();
394:       const currentTracks = state.tracks;
395:       const currentSelectedTrackId = state.selectedTrackId;
396:       const currentEnvelopeLocked = state.envelopeLocked;
397: 
398:       const rect = container.getBoundingClientRect();
399:       
400:       // Attempt to get offset from shadow DOM
401:       let tracksOffsetTop = 0;
402:       const firstChild = container.firstChild;
403:       
404:       if (firstChild instanceof HTMLElement && firstChild.shadowRoot) {
405:         const scrollContainer = firstChild.shadowRoot.querySelector('.scroll');
406:         if (scrollContainer) {
407:           const canvases = scrollContainer.querySelector('.canvases');
408:           if (canvases) {
409:             const canvasesRect = canvases.getBoundingClientRect();
410:             tracksOffsetTop = canvasesRect.top - rect.top;
411:           }
412:         }
413:       } else if (container.shadowRoot) {
414:         const canvases = container.shadowRoot.querySelector('.canvases');
415:         if (canvases) {
416:           const canvasesRect = canvases.getBoundingClientRect();
417:           tracksOffsetTop = canvasesRect.top - rect.top;
418:         }
419:       }
420: 
421:       const y = e.clientY - rect.top - tracksOffsetTop;
422:       
423:       let trackIndex = -1;
424:       let currentY = 0;
425:       const rootStyle = getComputedStyle(document.documentElement);
426:       const expandedH = parseInt(rootStyle.getPropertyValue('--expanded-track-h')) || 80;
427:       const normalH = parseInt(rootStyle.getPropertyValue('--normal-track-h')) || 50;
428: 
429:       const trackList = currentTracks || [];
430:       for (let i = 0; i < trackList.length; i++) {
431:         const h = (trackList[i].id === currentSelectedTrackId && !currentEnvelopeLocked) ? expandedH : normalH;
432:         if (y >= currentY && y < currentY + h) {
433:           trackIndex = i;
434:           break;
435:         }
436:         currentY += h;
437:       }
438:       
439:       if (trackIndex < 0 || trackIndex >= trackList.length) return;
440:       
441:       const clickedTrack = trackList[trackIndex];
442:       const currentTime = state.getMultitrackTime();
443:       
444:       // Find which phrase is at the current time
445:       let clickedPhraseId = null;
446:       for (const p of clickedTrack.phrases) {
447:         const visualStart = p.startPosition;
448:         const visualEnd = p.startPosition + (p.endCue || p.duration) - (p.startCue || 0);
449:         
450:         // Use a slightly larger buffer for double-clicks
451:         if (currentTime >= visualStart - 0.1 && currentTime <= visualEnd + 0.1) {
452:           clickedPhraseId = p.id;
453:           break;
454:         }
455:       }
456:       
457:       if (clickedPhraseId) {
458:         state.setSelectedPhraseId(clickedPhraseId);
459:         state.setSelectedTrackId(clickedTrack.id);
460:       } else {
461:         state.setSelectedPhraseId(null);
462:         state.setSelectedTrackId(clickedTrack.id);
463:       }
464:     };
465: 
466:     container.addEventListener('dblclick', handleDoubleClick);
467:     return () => {
468:       container.removeEventListener('dblclick', handleDoubleClick);
469:       container.removeEventListener('wheel', handleWheel);
470:     };
471:   }, [containerRef, multitrackRef]);
472: 
473: 
474:   const handlePlayPause = () => {
475:     playPause();
476:   };
477: 
478:   const handleStop = () => {
479:     setIsPlaying(false);
480:     if (isRecording) {
481:       stopRecording();
482:     }
483:     seekTo(0);
484:   };
485: 
486:   const handleRecord = async (trackId: string) => {
487:     if (isRecording) {
488:       perfLogger.log(8);
489:       stopRecording();
490:       return;
491:     }
492:     
493:     setSelectedTrackId(trackId);
494:     
495:     if (trackId === 'metronome') {
496:       alert("Recording on the metronome track is not allowed.");
497:       return;
498:     }
499:     
500:     perfLogger.log(7, trackId);
501:     startRecording(trackId);
502:   };
503: 
504:   const handleTrackPointerDown = (trackId: string) => {
505:     if (trackId === 'metronome') return;
506:     longPressTimer.current = setTimeout(() => {
507:       setEditingTrackId(trackId);
508:     }, 800); // Increased to 800ms to avoid conflict with double-tap
509:   };
510: 
511:   const handleMutePointerDown = (e: React.PointerEvent, trackId: string) => {
512:     e.stopPropagation();
513:     muteLongPressTimer.current[trackId] = setTimeout(() => {
514:       const track = tracks.find(t => t.id === trackId);
515:       if (track) {
516:         updateTrack(trackId, { isSolo: !track.isSolo });
517:         muteLongPressTimer.current[trackId] = null;
518:       }
519:     }, 500);
520:   };
521: 
522:   const handleMutePointerUp = (e: React.PointerEvent, trackId: string, isMuted: boolean) => {
523:     e.stopPropagation();
524:     if (muteLongPressTimer.current[trackId]) {
525:       clearTimeout(muteLongPressTimer.current[trackId]!);
526:       muteLongPressTimer.current[trackId] = null;
527:       // Short tap: toggle mute
528:       updateTrack(trackId, { isMuted: !isMuted });
529:     }
530:   };
531: 
532:   const handleTrackPointerUp = (trackId: string) => {
533:     if (longPressTimer.current) {
534:       clearTimeout(longPressTimer.current);
535:       longPressTimer.current = null;
536:       
537:       // It was a tap, not a long press
538:       const now = Date.now();
539:       const lastTap = lastTapTime.current[trackId] || 0;
540:       
541:       if (now - lastTap < 300) {
542:         // Double tap - select track and open settings
543:         setSelectedTrackId(trackId);
544:         setEditingTrackId(trackId);
545:         lastTapTime.current[trackId] = 0; // reset
546:       } else {
547:         // Single tap
548:         setSelectedTrackId(trackId);
549:         lastTapTime.current[trackId] = now;
550:       }
551:     }
552:   };
553: 
554:   const handleTrackPointerLeave = () => {
555:     if (longPressTimer.current) {
556:       clearTimeout(longPressTimer.current);
557:       longPressTimer.current = null;
558:     }
559:   };
560: 
561:   const handleFileImport = async (e: React.ChangeEvent<HTMLInputElement>) => {
562:     const file = e.target.files?.[0];
563:     if (!file) return;
564:     try {
565:       const data = await importProjectFromJSON(file);
566:       // Restore the store heavily via localforage override or explicit set
567:       useStore.setState({
568:         tracks: data.tracks || [],
569:         cues: data.cues || [],
570:         bpm: data.bpm || 120,
571:         timeSignature: data.timeSignature || [4, 4],
572:         globalLatencyMs: data.globalLatencyMs || 0,
573:         lyricsText: data.lyricsText || '',
574:         lyricsSegments: data.lyricsSegments || [],
575:       });
576:       alert('Project loaded from file!');
577:     } catch (err) {
578:       console.error(err);
579:       alert('Failed to parse file.');
580:     }
581:   };
582: 
583:   return (
584:     <div className="h-screen bg-zinc-950 text-zinc-100 flex flex-col font-sans selection:bg-zinc-800 overflow-hidden">
585:       <StatusLogger />
586:       
587:       {/* Hidden input for importing project JSON file */}
588:       <input 
589:         type="file" 
590:         accept=".awnn,.json" 
591:         className="hidden" 
592:         ref={fileInputRef} 
593:         onChange={handleFileImport} 
594:       />
595: 
596:       {/* Loading Overlay */}
597:       {!isReady && (
598:         <div className="fixed inset-0 z-[1000] bg-zinc-950 flex flex-col items-center justify-center gap-4">
599:           <div className="w-12 h-12 border-4 border-zinc-800 border-t-zinc-100 rounded-full animate-spin" />
600:           <div className="flex flex-col items-center gap-1">
601:             <h2 className="text-lg font-semibold tracking-tight">Initializing Engine</h2>
602:             <p className="text-xs text-zinc-500 font-mono uppercase tracking-widest">Preparing Audio Worklets & Waveforms</p>
603:           </div>
604:         </div>
605:       )}
606:       
607:       {/* Combined Header & Transport */}
608:       <header className={cn(
609:         "border-b border-zinc-800 bg-zinc-900/90 flex shrink-0 z-50 w-full overflow-hidden",
610:         isSmallPortrait ? "flex-col h-20" : "h-10 sm:h-12 items-center px-2 sm:px-4"
611:       )}>
612:         {isSmallPortrait ? (
613:           /* Small Portrait: 3-Area Layout (2 rows) */
614:           <>
615:             {/* Row 1 & 2 Container with 3 areas */}
616:             <div className="flex-1 flex items-stretch">
617:               {/* Left Area: 2 rows of items */}
618:               <div className="flex flex-col justify-between py-1 px-2 gap-0.5">
619:                   <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
620:                   <button onClick={() => exportProjectToJSON(useStore.getState())} className={cn(getBtnClass(true), "rounded transition-colors text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-100 hover:bg-zinc-800")} title="Export"><Save size={headerIconSize} /></button>
621:                   <button onClick={() => fileInputRef.current?.click()} className={cn(getBtnClass(true), "rounded transition-colors text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-100 hover:bg-zinc-800")} title="Import"><FolderOpen size={headerIconSize} /></button>
622:                   <button onClick={() => setShowMetronomeSettings(true)} className={cn(getBtnClass(true), "rounded transition-colors flex items-center gap-1")} title="Metronome">
623:                     <Volume2 size={headerIconSize} className={cn(tracks.find(t => t.id === 'metronome')?.isMuted && "opacity-50")} />
624:                     <span className="text-[9px] font-bold uppercase">{mainToolbarLabel('Metro', 'M')}</span>
625:                   </button>
626:                 </div>
627:                 <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
628:                   <button onClick={() => setAppMode(appMode === 'mixer' ? 'lyrics' : 'mixer')} className={cn(getBtnClass(), "rounded text-xs font-semibold bg-zinc-800 text-white")}>
629:                     {appMode === 'mixer' ? mainToolbarLabel('Lyrics', 'Lyrics') : mainToolbarLabel('Multitrack', 'Mix')}
630:                   </button>
631:                   {appMode === 'mixer' && (
632:                     <>
633:                       <button onClick={() => setMoveLocked(!moveLocked)} className={cn(getBtnClass(true), moveLocked ? "text-red-400 bg-red-500/10" : "text-green-400 bg-green-500/10")} title={moveLocked ? "Locked" : "Unlocked"}>
634:                         {moveLocked ? <Lock size={headerIconSize} /> : <Unlock size={headerIconSize} />}
635:                       </button>
636:                       <button onClick={() => setEnvelopeLocked(!envelopeLocked)} className={cn(getBtnClass(true), envelopeLocked ? "text-red-400 bg-red-500/10" : "text-green-400 bg-green-500/10")} title={envelopeLocked ? "Locked" : "Unlocked"}>
637:                         <Activity size={headerIconSize} />
638:                       </button>
639:                     </>
640:                   )}
641:                 </div>
642:               </div>
643: 
644:               {/* Center Area: Transport controls (spans 2 rows, bigger) */}
645:               <div className="flex-1 flex items-center justify-center gap-2 px-4">
646:                 <button onClick={() => seekTo(0)} className={cn(getBtnClass(true), "text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-100 rounded-full")}><Rewind size={headerIconSize} /></button>
647:                 <button onClick={handleStop} className={cn(getBtnClass(true), "text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-100 rounded-full")}><Square size={headerIconSize} fill="currentColor" /></button>
648:                 <button onClick={handlePlayPause} className={cn("w-12 h-12 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center", isPlaying ? "bg-zinc-100 text-zinc-900" : "bg-zinc-800 text-zinc-100 border border-zinc-700")}>
649:                   {isPlaying ? <Pause size={20} fill="currentColor" /> : <Play size={20} className="ml-0.5" fill="currentColor" />}
650:                 </button>
651:                 <button onClick={() => seekTo(useStore.getState().currentTime + 10)} className={cn(getBtnClass(true), "text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-100 rounded-full")}><FastForward size={headerIconSize} /></button>
652:               </div>
653: 
654:               {/* Right Area: 2 rows of items */}
655:               <div className="flex flex-col justify-between py-1 px-2 gap-0.5">
656:                 <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
657:                   <button onClick={() => {
658:                     const modes = ['always', 'recording', 'none'] as const;
659:                     const nextMode = modes[(modes.indexOf(preRollMode) + 1) % modes.length];
660:                     setPreRollMode(nextMode);
661:                   }} className={cn(getBtnClass(), "rounded text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-100 hover:bg-zinc-800")} title={`Pre-roll: ${preRollMode}`}>
662:                     <span className="text-[9px] font-bold uppercase">{mainToolbarLabel(preRollMode, preRollMode.substring(0, 3))}</span>
663:                   </button>
664:                   <button onClick={() => setShowCues(!showCues)} className={cn(getBtnClass(true), "rounded", showCues ? "bg-zinc-800 text-zinc-100" : "text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-100 hover:bg-zinc-800")} title="Cues">
665:                     <Music size={headerIconSize} />
666:                   </button>
667:                   <button onClick={() => setShowSettings(!showSettings)} className={cn(getBtnClass(true), "rounded text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-100 hover:bg-zinc-800")} title="Settings">
668:                     <Settings size={headerIconSize} />
669:                   </button>
670:                 </div>
671:                 <div className="flex items-center gap-1">
672:                   <div className="flex items-center gap-1 px-1">
673:                     <span className="text-[9px] font-bold text-zinc-500 uppercase">{mainToolbarLabel('Tempo', 'BPM')}</span>
674:                     <span className="text-[9px] font-mono font-bold text-zinc-300">{bpm}</span>
675:                     <span className="text-[9px] font-bold text-zinc-500">|</span>
676:                     <span className="text-[9px] font-mono font-bold text-zinc-300">{timeSignature[0]}/{timeSignature[1]}</span>
677:                   </div>
678:                 </div>
679:               </div>
680:             </div>
681:           </>
682:         ) : (
683:           /* Normal Layout (landscape/medium/large) */
684:           <>
685:             {/* Left: Logo & Project Actions */}
686:             <div className="flex-1 flex items-center gap-2 sm:gap-4 justify-start overflow-hidden">
687:               <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
688:                 <div className="w-7 h-7 sm:w-8 sm:h-8 rounded-lg bg-zinc-800 flex items-center justify-center border border-zinc-700">
689:                   <Music className="w-3.5 h-3.5 sm:w-4 sm:h-4 text-zinc-400" />
690:                 </div>
691:                 <h1 className="font-semibold text-xs sm:text-sm tracking-wide hidden lg:block">AWNN</h1>
692:               </div>
693:               <div className="hidden sm:flex items-center gap-1">
694:                 <button onClick={() => exportProjectToJSON(useStore.getState())} className={cn(getBtnClass(true), "rounded transition-colors text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-100 hover:bg-zinc-800")} title="Export Project File"><Save size={headerIconSize} /></button>
695:                 <button onClick={() => fileInputRef.current?.click()} className={cn(getBtnClass(true), "rounded transition-colors text-zinc-400 hover:text-zinc-100 hover:bg-zinc-800")} title="Import Project File"><FolderOpen size={headerIconSize} /></button>
696:               </div>
697:

701:                   onClick={() => setAppMode(appMode === 'mixer' ? 'lyrics' : 'mixer')}
702:                   className="px-3 py-1 flex items-center gap-2 rounded text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider transition-all bg-zinc-800 text-white hover:bg-zinc-700 shadow-sm"
703:                   title={appMode === 'mixer' ? "Switch to Lyrics Mode" : "Switch to Multitrack Mode"}
704:                 >
705:                   {appMode === 'mixer' ? (
706:                     <><FileText size={14} /> {mainToolbarLabel('Lyrics', 'Lyrics')}</>
707:                   ) : (
708:                     <><Mic size={14} /> {mainToolbarLabel('Multitrack', 'Mix')}</>
709:                   )}
710:                 </button>
711:               </div>
712:

714:               <div className="flex items-center gap-2 ml-2 border-l border-zinc-800 pl-4">

717:                   className="flex items-center gap-2 px-2 py-1 hover:bg-zinc-800 rounded transition-colors group"
718:                   title="Metronome & Grid Settings"
719:                 >
720:                   <div className="flex flex-col items-start">
721:                     <span className="text-[10px] font-bold text-zinc-500 uppercase tracking-tighter leading-none">{mainToolbarLabel('Tempo', 'Tempo')}</span>
722:                     <span className="text-xs font-mono font-bold text-zinc-300 group-hover:text-white transition-colors">{bpm} BPM</span>
723:                   </div>

725:                   <div className="flex flex-col items-start">
726:                     <span className="text-[10px] font-bold text-zinc-500 uppercase tracking-tighter leading-none">Sig</span>
727:                     <span className="text-xs font-mono font-bold text-zinc-300 group-hover:text-white transition-colors">{timeSignature[0]}/{timeSignature[1]}</span>
728:                   </div>

730:                 
731:                 {/* Metronome Mute Toggle */}

736:                       const metronome = tracks.find(t => t.id === 'metronome');

745:                     )}
746:                     title="Toggle Metronome"

882:                       {cue.label}
883:                     </div>

886:                 ))}
887:               </div>

928:                     </div>
929:                     <div className="flex items-center gap-2">

931:                       <button

934:                           removeCue(cue.id);

937:                       >
938:                         <Trash2 className="w-3 h-3" />
939:                       </button>
940:                     </div>
941:                   </div>
942:                 ))
943:               )}

945: 
946:           </aside>

971:             </div>
972: 
973:             <div className="p-4 overflow-y-auto space-y-6">
974:               <div>

976:                   Microphone Capture

980:                     <div className="text-sm font-bold text-zinc-100">Raw Capture</div>
981:                     <div className="text-[10px] text-zinc-500 mt-1 leading-tight">Disables browser echo cancellation, noise suppression, and AGC. Allows for lower latency, but requires headphones to avoid feedback.</div>
982:                   </div>
983:                   <button 
984:                     onClick={() => useStore.getState().setRawRecordingMode(!useStore.getState().rawRecordingMode)}
985:                     className={cn("w-10 h-6 rounded-full relative transition-colors shrink-0", useStore.getState().rawRecordingMode ? "bg-green-500" : "bg-zinc-700")}

990:

1005:                   />
```

## Assistant (Plan · Ring 2.6 1T Free · 149.6s)

_Thinking:_

Let me analyze the current state of the codebase and the bugs that need fixing. Based on the progress summary and my file reads, here are the key issues:

1. **Worklet `_flush()` returns wrong frame**: It returns `_recordingStartFrame` (physical capture start) instead of `_anchoredFrame` (the sync anchor passed from main thread).

2. **Rolling buffer empty on "none" mode**: When preRollMode is "none", recording starts at the same frame as capture, so `rollingOffset=0`. The worklet needs to delay actual capture by `headLength` seconds so the rolling buffer fills during the delay.

3. **`punchInUserTime_Real` uses absolute AudioContext time**: The computation `audioCtx.currentTime + delay` gives absolute time, but it should compute a timeline-relative anchor frame: `anchorFrame = floor((punchInUserTime + secondsPerBar) * sampleRate)`.

4. **No audio playback on recorded clips**: `onAddPhrase` → `addTrack()` nests audio data inside `phrases[]`, but `multitrack.initAudio()` reads from top-level `track.audioBuffer`/`track.url`. The data needs to be flattened.

5. **Duplicate gain node connections**: The muted setter in WebAudioPlayer reconnects without checking if already connected.

6. **Missing `onSetIsRecording(false)` after recording stop** in the non-cancelled path.

Let me now plan the specific changes needed for each file:

### 1. `public/worklets/recorder.worklet.js`
- In the `START_RECORDING` handler: accept `anchoredFrame` from main thread instead of computing it from `punchInUserTime_Real`. For "none" mode with delayed recording, schedule `_isRecording = true` at `currentFrame + headLength * sampleRate`.
- In `_flush()`: return `_anchoredFrame` instead of `_recordingStartFrame`.
- For "none" mode: delay setting `_isRecording = true` so the rolling buffer fills during the head period.

### 2. `src/audio/recording/RecordingEngine.ts`
- Compute `anchorFrame = Math.floor((punchInUserTime + secondsPerBar) * sampleRate)` in main thread.
- Remove the blocking `await setTimeout(1000)` for "none" mode - let worklet handle the delay.
- Send `anchoredFrame` to worklet instead of `punchInUserTime_Real`.
- Remove `punchInUserTime_Real` computation (replace with anchorFrame).

### 3. `src/hooks/useAudioEngine.ts`

1. Accept `anchoredFrame` from the main thread message
2. For "none" mode, schedule `_isRecording = true` after `headLength * sampleRate` frames
3. `_flush()` should return `_anchoredFrame`

For RecordingEngine:
1. Compute anchorFrame in main thread: `Math.floor((punchInUserTime + secondsPerBar) * sampleRate)`

Wait, let me re-read the worklet more carefully. In the current code:

```javascript
this.port.onmessage = (event) => {
  if (event.data.type === 'START_RECORDING') {
    this._shouldStop = false;
    if (event.data.punchInUserTime_Real !== undefined) {
      this._anchoredFrame = Math.floor(event.data.punchInUserTime_Real * sampleRate);
      this._isRecording = true;
      this._audioData = [];
      this._recordingStartFrame = currentFrame;
    }
  }
};
```

The main thread currently computes `punchInUserTime_Real = audioContext.currentTime + startupDelay + timeFromPlaybackStartToPunchIn` (an absolute AudioContext time), and the worklet converts it to a frame number. But this is problematic because `punchInUserTime_Real` is an absolute time, not timeline-relative.

The fix should be:
- Main thread computes `anchorFrame = Math.floor((punchInUserTime + secondsPerBar) * sampleRate)` and sends it
- Worklet uses that directly as `_anchoredFrame`
- For "none" mode, delay actual recording start by `headLength * sampleRate` frames

Let me also understand the "none" mode fix better. In "none" pre-roll mode:
- The user presses record while transport is already playing (or starts playing immediately)
- There's no count-in bar, so we need to capture audio BEFORE the punch-in point
- The worklet should start capturing to the rolling buffer immediately, but set `_isRecording = true` only after `headLength * sampleRate` frames
- This way, the rolling buffer accumulates `headLength` seconds of audio before the actual recording starts
- When flushed, the rolling buffer data before `_recordingStartFrame` becomes the "head" of the clip

Let me now write up the precise changes.

For the worklet `recorder.worklet.js`:

```javascript
// In onmessage handler:
if (event.data.type === 'START_RECORDING') {
  this._shouldStop = false;
  if (event.data.anchoredFrame !== undefined) {
    this._anchoredFrame = event.data.anchoredFrame;
  }
  
  // For "none" pre-roll mode, delay actual recording to allow rolling buffer to fill
  const captureDelayFrames = event.data.captureDelayFrames || 0;
  
  this._audioData = [];
  this._recordingStartFrame = currentFrame + captureDelayFrames;
  
  if (captureDelayFrames > 0) {
    // Schedule recording start after delay
    setTimeout(() => {
      this._isRecording = true;
    }, captureDelayFrames / sampleRate * 1000);
  } else {
    this._isRecording = true;
  }
}
```

Wait, but AudioWorklet's `setTimeout` is not guaranteed to be sample-accurate. Better to use `currentFrame` tracking:

Actually, looking at this more carefully, I think the right approach is:

1. For "none" mode: immediately start capturing to rolling buffer (which is already happening), but delay `_isRecording = true` by scheduling it based on frame count.
2. We can track it in `process()`: check if `currentFrame >= this._recordingStartFrame` to start actual recording.

Let me revise:

```javascript
// In constructor:
this._recordingStartFrame = 0;

// In onmessage:
if (event.data.type === 'START_RECORDING') {
  this._shouldStop = false;
  this._anchoredFrame = event.data.anchoredFrame || 0;
  this._audioData = [];
  // For "none" mode, captureDelayFrames > 0 means we wait that many frames before recording
  this._recordingStartFrame = currentFrame + (event.data.captureDelayFrames || 0);
  this._isRecording = (event.data.captureDelayFrames || 0) === 0;
}

// In process():
if (!this._isRecording && currentFrame >= this._recordingStartFrame) {
  this._isRecording = true;
}
```

This way:
- Rolling buffer always captures from frame 0 (when process starts)
- `_isRecording` becomes true only after `captureDelayFrames` have elapsed
- When flushed, the rolling buffer data before `_recordingStartFrame` becomes the "head"
- `_recordingStartFrame` is used in `_flush()` to split the rolling buffer

For `_flush()`:
```javascript
_flush() {
  // Use _recordingStartFrame for splitting the rolling buffer
  const recordStartFrame = this._recordingStartFrame;
  // ... existing rolling buffer logic ...
  
  // Return _anchoredFrame instead of _recordingStartFrame
  return [result, this._anchoredFrame, rollingOffset];
}
```

Now for `RecordingEngine.ts`:

```typescript
async startRecording(trackId: string): Promise<void> {
  // ... existing setup ...
  
  // Compute anchorFrame in main thread
  const sampleRate = audioContext.sampleRate;
  const beatsPerSecond = this.config.bpm / 60;
  const secondsPerBeat = 1 / beatsPerSecond;
  const secondsPerBar = secondsPerBeat * this.config.timeSignature[0];
  
  // anchorFrame is the frame offset from UserTime 0 where the definitive audio starts
  // This is computed as: (punchInUserTime + secondsPerBar) * sampleRate
  // For "none" mode: punchInUserTime is where the user pressed record
  // For pre-roll modes: punchInUserTime is the start of Bar 1
  const anchorFrame = Math.floor((punchInUserTime + secondsPerBar) * sampleRate);
  
  // For "none" mode, calculate capture delay to fill the rolling buffer
  const captureDelayFrames = effectivePreRollMode === 'none' 
    ? Math.floor(this.headLength * sampleRate) 
    : 0;
  
  this.audioWorkletNode.port.postMessage({ 
    type: 'START_RECORDING',
    anchoredFrame: anchorFrame,
    captureDelayFrames: captureDelayFrames
  });
  
  // Remove the blocking await setTimeout(1000)
}
```

Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the "none" pre-roll mode. Looking at the current flow:

For "none" mode (not currently playing):
1. User presses record
2. `punchInUserTime = currentTime` (store state)
3. `recordStartUserTime = punchInUserTime` (no pre-roll subtraction)
4. Seek to `recordStartUserTime`
5. Start playback
6. Worklet starts recording immediately

The problem: When playback starts from `recordStartUserTime`, the audio context time needs to advance to the punch-in point. During this advancement, the rolling buffer captures audio, but since `_isRecording = true` from the start, everything captured goes into `_audioData`, not just the definitive part.

For "none" mode, what we want:
1. Start capturing to rolling buffer immediately (to get the pre-roll audio)
2. Delay `_isRecording` by `headLength` seconds so the definitive recording starts at the right time
3. On flush, the rolling buffer data before `_recordingStartFrame` becomes the head

So the capture delay for "none" mode should be `headLength * sampleRate` frames.

Now let me also think about the `onAddPhrase` → `addTrack()` data flattening issue.

Currently in `useAudioEngine.ts`, the `onAddPhrase` callback does:
```typescript
multitrackRef.current.addTrack(trackOptions);
```

Where `trackOptions` has `phrases: [{ url, audioBuffer, peaks, ... }]`.

But `multitrack.addTrack` → `initAudio` expects:
```typescript
if (track.audioBuffer && audio instanceof WebAudioPlayer && track.url) {
  audio.loadBuffer(track.url, track.audioBuffer)
} else if (track.url) {
  audio.src = track.url
}
```

It reads `track.url` and `track.audioBuffer` from the top level, not from phrases. When adding a track with phrases, the audio data is in `phrases[0].url` and `phrases[0].audioBuffer`, but `initAudio` can't find `track.url`.

So the fix is: In the trackOptions passed to `addTrack`, flatten the first phrase's audio data to the top level.

Let me now check the `addTrack` method more carefully in multitrack.ts:

```typescript
public addTrack(track: TrackOptions) {
  let index = this.tracks.findIndex((t) => t.id === track.id)
  if (index !== -1) {
    this.tracks[index] = track
    this.initAudio(track).then((audio) => {
      // ...
    })
  }
}
```

And `initAudio`:
```typescript
private initAudio(track: TrackOptions): Promise<HTMLAudioElement | WebAudioPlayer> {
  let audio;
  if (track.anchoredFrame !== undefined && track.anchoredFrame !== 0) {
    audio = new WebAudioPlayer(this.audioContext, { anchoredFrame: track.anchoredFrame, headLength: track.headLength || 1.0 });
  } else {
    audio = (track.options?.media as HTMLAudioElement | WebAudioPlayer) || new WebAudioPlayer(this.audioContext, { headLength: 0 });
  }
  
  audio.crossOrigin = 'anonymous'
  
  if (track.audioBuffer && audio instanceof WebAudioPlayer && track.url) {
    audio.loadBuffer(track.url, track.audioBuffer)
  } else if (track.url) {
    audio.src = track.url
  }
}
```

So `initAudio` needs `track.url`, `track.audioBuffer`, or `track.options.media`. When the track has phrases, we need to use the first phrase's audio data.

The fix in `useAudioEngine.ts` should create the trackOptions with top-level audio data from the first phrase:

```typescript
const firstPhrase = track.phrases[0];
const trackOptions = {
  id: track.id,
  name: track.name,
  color: track.color,
  isMuted: track.isMuted,
  isSolo: track.isSolo,
  volume: track.isMuted ? 0 : track.volume,
  pan: track.pan,
  offset: track.offset,
  anchoredFrame: track.anchoredFrame,
  // Top-level audio data from first phrase
  url: firstPhrase.url,
  audioBuffer: firstPhrase.audioBuffer,
  peaks: firstPhrase.peaks,
  headLength: firstPhrase.headLength,
  originalAnchoredFrame: firstPhrase.originalAnchoredFrame,
  startPosition: isMetronome ? track.phrases[0].startPosition : track.phrases[0].startPosition + secondsPerBar + trackOffset,
  // ... rest
};
```

Wait, but the track's `phrases` array in the trackOptions is also set. Let me look at what `addTrack` does with it... Actually in `addTrack`, the entire track object is stored: `this.tracks[index] = track`. Then `initAudio(track)` is called which only looks at top-level properties.

So the key fix is: put the audio data at top level so `initAudio` can find it.

Actually, looking at the muted setter more carefully:
```typescript
set muted(value: boolean) {
  if (this._muted === value) return
  this._muted = value
  if (this._muted) {
    this.gainNode.disconnect()
  } else {
    if (!this.gainNode.context?.destination) {
      this.gainNode.connect(this.audioContext.destination)
    }
  }
}
```

The problem: `this.gainNode.context.destination` refers to the AudioContext's destination (the speakers), which is always truthy. So the reconnect condition `if (!this.gainNode.context?.destination)` is always false, and the gain node is never reconnected after being muted. The fix: track the connection state ourselves or use a different check.

Simple fix:
```typescript
set muted(value: boolean) {
  if (this._muted === value) return
  this._muted = value
  if (this._muted) {
    this.gainNode.disconnect()
  } else {
    this.gainNode.connect(this.audioContext.destination)
  }
}
```

### Changes needed:

**1. `public/worklets/recorder.worklet.js`**
- Accept `anchoredFrame` and `captureDelayFrames` from main thread message
- Delay setting `_isRecording = true` based on `captureDelayFrames`
- In `_flush()`, return `_anchoredFrame` instead of `_recordingStartFrame`
- Add in-process check for delayed recording start

**2. `src/audio/recording/RecordingEngine.ts`**
- Replace `punchInUserTime_Real` computation with `anchorFrame` calculation: `Math.floor((punchInUserTime + secondsPerBar) * sampleRate)`
- Send `{ anchoredFrame, captureDelayFrames }` to worklet instead of `punchInUserTime_Real`
- Remove blocking `await setTimeout(1000)` for "none" mode
- Set `captureDelayFrames` based on mode: `headLength * sampleRate` for "none", 0 otherwise
- Fix `handleAudioWorkletStop` to use `anchorFrame` directly

**3. `src/hooks/useAudioEngine.ts`**
- Remove the direct `multitrackRef.current.addTrack()` call from `onAddPhrase`
- Let the App.tsx `syncedTrackIdsRef` effect handle track addition

**4. `src/App.tsx`**

1. **Worklet `_flush()` returns wrong frame**: It returns `_recordingStartFrame` (physical capture start) instead of `_anchoredFrame` (the sync anchor passed from main thread).

2. **Rolling buffer empty on "none" mode**: `rollingOffset=0` because recording starts at same frame as capture - no head accumulated. Need to delay actual capture by `headLength` seconds.

3. **`punchInUserTime_Real` uses absolute AudioContext time**: Needs to be timeline-relative. Should compute `anchorFrame = floor((punchInUserTime + secondsPerBar) * sampleRate)` in main thread.

Actually, I realize the chunk size depends on the render quantum. At 44100Hz with 128 sample frames, each chunk has 128 samples. So for a chunk at frame F, its chunkEndFrame = F + 128.

The earliest chunk (1 second before recording) is at frame X-343*128 = X-43904. Its chunkEndFrame = X-43904+128 = X-43776. That's way before X, so it should be included.

The last chunk before recording starts is at frame X-128 (one quantum before recording starts at frame X). chunkEndFrame = X-128+128 = X. So chunkEndFrame <= recordStartFrame (=X) → include.

Then the chunk at frame X has chunk.frame = X = recordStartFrame. Since chunkEndFrame = X+128 > X, and chunk.frame = X is NOT < X, we break.

Actually wait - the physical frame counter in an AudioWorklet IS the same as AudioContext.currentTime * sampleRate. So `currentFrame` at time 52s with 44100 sampleRate = 52 * 44100 = 2292000.

The key insight from the summary: **`anchoredFrame` should represent the timeline-relative sync point, NOT absolute AudioContext time — computed as `floor((punchInUserTime + secondsPerBar) * sampleRate)` and passed to the worklet (not computed inside it)**

So the fix is:
1. In RecordingEngine: compute `anchorFrame = Math.floor((punchInUserTime + secondsPerBar) * sampleRate)`
2. Send `anchorFrame` to the worklet

### recorder.worklet.js changes:

```js
// In onmessage handler:
if (event.data.type === 'START_RECORDING') {
  this._shouldStop = false;

    this._anchoredFrame = event.data.anchoredFrame;
  }

}
```

Let me revise:


This way:
- Rolling buffer always captures (process always adds to rolling buffer)

- `_flush()` correctly splits at `_recordingStartFrame`

```ts
});
```

In this case, the rolling buffer captures audio from frame F (start of playback+recording). The recording also starts at frame F. So `_recordingStartFrame = F` and all rolling buffer chunks have `chunk.frame >= F`. In _flush, the loop breaks immediately → rollingOffset = 0 → no head.

But audioData contains ALL the audio: pre-roll + actual recording. And `_recordingStartFrame = F` means the audioData starts at F, which is also the start of the pre-roll.

So the resulting audio is: no head from rolling buffer + all audio from audioData. The audioData includes the pre-roll portion.

But that means the audio includes the count-in/pre-roll audio, and the startPosition is calculated as:
```ts
startPos = (anchorFrame / sampleRate) - secondsPerBar - headLength
```

For "always" mode: anchorFrame is computed as `floor((punchInUserTime + secondsPerBar) * sampleRate)`. So:
```
startPos = (punchInUserTime + secondsPerBar) - secondsPerBar - headLength
        = punchInUserTime - headLength
```

For default headLength = 1s, startPos = punchInUserTime - 1.

And the audio buffer contains: [pre-roll (secondsPerBar long)] + [actual recording]. The total duration is secondsPerBar + actual recording duration.

The visual clip starts at startPos (punchInUserTime - 1) and has duration = secondsPerBar + actualRecordingDuration. The clip spans from punchInUserTime - 1 to punchInUserTime + actualRecordingDuration.

The "head" (first 1 second) is the pre-roll, and the "definitive" recording starts at punchInUserTime. This aligns with the startPosition being punchInUserTime - headLength.

OK, so for "always" mode, the current approach actually works correctly IF the audioData contains the pre-roll+recording. The rolling buffer isn't needed for "always" mode.

For "none" mode though, the current approach is broken:
1. No pre-roll playback
2. Recording starts immediately (no capture delay)
3. Rolling buffer has 1s of data from the initial setTimeout, but recording also captures from the same frame
4. _recordingStartFrame = currentFrame, so rolling buffer chunks with the same frame aren't included

The fix for "none" mode needs the worklet to delay recording by headLength while the rolling buffer fills. After headLength seconds:
- Rolling buffer has headLength of audio (the pre-roll)

- _recordingStartFrame = frame when recording actually started
- _flush: rolling buffer chunks before _recordingStartFrame are the head

```ts
  ...
}
```

```
349:         punchInUserTime = Math.max(0, storeState?.currentTime || 0);
350:       }
351: 
352:       const currentTime = punchInUserTime; // For compatibility with rest of function
353: 
354:       // Use storeTime for punchIn calculation
355:       // Increment session ID to invalidate any pending handleRecorderStop calls
356:       this.recordingSessionId++;
357:       const currentSessionId = this.recordingSessionId;
358:       perfLogger.log(18, trackId, currentSessionId);
359:       perfLogger.log(19, trackId, currentSessionId);
360: 
361:       // Ensure stream exists (will call initStream to initialize AudioWorklet)
362:       if (!this.continuousMicStream) {
363:         await this.initStream();
364:         perfLogger.log(20, this.recordingSessionId, 0);
365:       }
366: 
367:       const preRollMode = this.config.preRollMode;
368:       perfLogger.log(21, preRollMode, currentTime);
369: 
370:       // If already playing, force no count-in regardless of preRollMode setting
371:       const effectivePreRollMode = isCurrentlyPlaying ? 'none' : preRollMode;
372: 
373:       if (currentTime < 0) {
374:         alert("Cannot start recording during the pre-roll (before Bar 1).");
375:         return;
376:       }
377: 
378:       // Store punchInUserTime for clip startPos
379:       this.punchInUserTime = punchInUserTime;
380: 
381:       // Set activeTrackId early so stop handlers can reference it
382:       this.activeTrackId = trackId;
383: 
384:       // For "none" + not playing: prime the buffer (wait 1s for 2s rolling buffer to fill)
385:       if (!isCurrentlyPlaying && effectivePreRollMode === 'none') {
386:         await new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 1000));
387:       }
388: 
389:       // Calculate record start time (for seeking to pre-roll start)
390:       const recordStartUserTime = effectivePreRollMode !== 'none' ? punchInUserTime - secondsPerBar : punchInUserTime;
391: 
392:       // Seek to pre-roll start (allow negative user time for pre-roll)
393:       // Only seek if we are NOT already playing (to avoid interrupting playback)
394:       if (!isCurrentlyPlaying) {
395:         this.callbacks.onSeekTo(recordStartUserTime, true);
396:       }
397: 
398:       this.recordingStartTransportTime = recordStartUserTime;
399:       this.expectedPreRoll = effectivePreRollMode !== 'none' ? secondsPerBar : 0;
400:       this.punchInTime = performance.now();
401: this.recordingCancelled = false;
402: 
403:       // Coordinated start time for perfect sync
404:       const audioContext = this.config.audioContextRef?.current;
405:       if (!audioContext) throw new Error('AudioContext not initialized');
406:       
407:       // FIXED: Calculate punchInUserTime_Real NOW (just before playback starts)
408:       // This ensures audioContext.currentTime is accurate
409:       let punchInUserTime_Real: number;
410:       
411:       if (isCurrentlyPlaying) {
412:         // Playing: currentTime IS the Real Time
413:         punchInUserTime_Real = audioContext.currentTime;
414:       } else {
415:         // Stopped: Playback will start after a short delay (seek + onSetIsPlaying)
416:         const startupDelay = 0.15; // Approximate delay until playback starts
417:         const timeFromPlaybackStartToPunchIn = punchInUserTime - recordStartUserTime;
418:         // punchInUserTime_Real = when punch-in will occur in AudioContext time
419:         punchInUserTime_Real = audioContext.currentTime + startupDelay + timeFromPlaybackStartToPunchIn;
420:       }
421:       
422:       if (!this.audioWorkletNode) {
423:         throw new Error('AudioWorklet not initialized. This should not happen.');
424:       }
425:       
426:       // AudioWorklet: send START_RECORDING with punchInUserTime_Real
427:       // AudioWorklet will start recording 1s before punchInUserTime (recHeadstart)
428:       const captureImmediately = effectivePreRollMode === 'none';
429:       this.audioWorkletNode.port.postMessage({ 
430:         type: 'START_RECORDING',
431:         punchInUserTime_Real: punchInUserTime_Real,
432:         captureImmediately: captureImmediately
433:       });
434:       
435:       const recorderStartCtxTime = audioContext.currentTime;
436:       const playbackStartCtxTime = recorderStartCtxTime + 0.15;
437:       const startDelay = playbackStartCtxTime - recorderStartCtxTime;
438:       
439:       // Store the expected pre-roll duration (1 bar + the start delay)
440:       this.expectedPreRoll = (effectivePreRollMode !== 'none' ? secondsPerBar : 0) + startDelay;
441:       
442:       // Start Playback
443:       this.callbacks.onSetIsPlaying(true);
444:       this.callbacks.onSetIsRecording(true);
445:       
446:     } catch (err) {
447:       console.error("Error accessing microphone:", err);
448:       alert("Could not access microphone.");
449:       this.callbacks.onSetIsRecording(false);
450:       perfLogger.log(25, this.recordingSessionId, 0);
451:     }
452:   }
453: 
454:   stopRecording(): void {
455:     console.log('stopRecording: called', new Error().stack);
456:     
457:     if (!this.audioWorkletNode) {
458:       console.warn('stopRecording: AudioWorklet not initialized');
459:       return;
460:     }
461:     
462:     // AudioWorklet: send STOP_RECORDING message
463:     console.log('AudioWorklet: posting STOP_RECORDING message');

465:   }
466: 
467:   cancelRecording(): void {

469:     this.recordingCancelled = true;
470:

472:       // AudioWorklet: send STOP_RECORDING message to discard data
473:       this.audioWorkletNode.port.postMessage({ type: 'STOP_RECORDING' });
474:     }
475:     this.callbacks.onSetIsRecording(false);
476:   }
477: 
478:   cleanup(): void {
479:     if (this.audioWorkletNode) {
480:       this.audioWorkletNode.port.close();
481:       this.audioWorkletNode.disconnect();
482:       this.audioWorkletNode = null;
483:     }
484:     if (this.mediaStreamSource) {
485:       this.mediaStreamSource.disconnect();
486:       this.mediaStreamSource = null;
487:     }
488: 
489:     if (this.continuousMicStream) {
490:       this.continuousMicStream.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop());
491:       this.continuousMicStream = null;
492:     }
493: 
494:     this.activeTrackId = null;
495:   }

```

```typescript
private initAudio(track: TrackOptions): Promise<HTMLAudioElement | WebAudioPlayer> {
     perfLogger.log(2, track.trackId || track.id);

     let audio: HTMLAudioElement | WebAudioPlayer;
     if (track.anchoredFrame !== undefined && track.anchoredFrame !== 0) {
       audio = new WebAudioPlayer(this.audioContext, { anchoredFrame: track.anchoredFrame, headLength: track.headLength || 1.0 });
     } else {
       // Tracks without anchoredFrame (e.g. metronome) should play from the start with no head offset
       audio = (track.options?.media as HTMLAudioElement | WebAudioPlayer) || new WebAudioPlayer(this.audioContext, { headLength: 0 });
     }

     audio.crossOrigin = 'anonymous'

     if (track.audioBuffer && audio instanceof WebAudioPlayer && track.url) {
       audio.loadBuffer(track.url, track.audioBuffer)
     } else if (track.url) {
       audio.src = track.url
     }
     ...
```

Now let me also check if the `startPositionsHash` effect in useAudioEngine correctly handles the conversion. Looking at the code:

```typescript
useEffect(() => {
     if (!multitrackRef.current || !isReady) return;
     
     let itemIndex = 0;
     
     (tracks || []).forEach(track => {
       if (track.phrases.length === 0) {
         itemIndex++;
       } else {
         track.phrases.forEach(phrase => {
           if (typeof multitrackRef.current?.setTrackStartPosition === 'function') {
             const isMetronome = track.id === 'metronome';
             const trackOffset = track.offset || 0;
             const realStartPosition = isMetronome ? phrase.startPosition : phrase.startPosition + secondsPerBar + trackOffset;
             try {
               multitrackRef.current.setTrackStartPosition(itemIndex, realStartPosition);
             } catch (e) {
               console.warn("Failed to set track start position:", e);
             }
           }
           itemIndex++;
         });
       }
     });
```

This converts UserTime → RealTime for `setTrackStartPosition`, which is correct because multitrack works in RealTime. Good.

```
1: /**
2:  * AudioWorklet processor for recording microphone audio
3:  * Uses AudioContext's clock (currentTime) for sample-accurate timing
4:  * Share the same clock as playback - symmetric jitter for better sync
5:  * Maintains a 2-second rolling buffer for pre-roll support
6:  */
7: class RecorderWorkletProcessor extends AudioWorkletProcessor {
8:   static get parameterDescriptors() {
9:     return [{
10:       name: 'isRecording',
11:       defaultValue: 0,
12:       minValue: 0,
13:       maxValue: 1,
14:     }];
15:   }
16: 
17:   constructor() {
18:     super();
19:     this._isRecording = false;
20:     this._audioData = []; // Array of Float32Array for current recording
21: 
22:     // Get headLength from processor options (passed from RecordingEngine)
23:     let headLength = 1.0;
24:     // Access processorOptions directly - no TypeScript syntax
25:     if (this.processorOptions && this.processorOptions.headLength !== undefined) {
26:       headLength = this.processorOptions.headLength;
27:     }
28:     // Rolling buffer should always be larger than headLength to work properly
29:     // Use at least 2 seconds or headLength + 1 second buffer
30:     this._rollingBufferDuration = Math.max(2.0, headLength + 1.0);
31:     this._recordingStartFrame = 0; // REAL TIME frame when actual audio capture began
32:     this._sampleRate = sampleRate;
33:     this._processCount = 0;
34:     this._shouldStop = false;
35:     this._anchoredFrame = 0; // Shared-clock frame of punch-in point (from main thread)
36:     this._headLength = headLength;
37: 
38:     this._rollingBuffer = []; // Initialize rolling buffer
39: 
40:     this.port.onmessage = (event) => {
41:       if (event.data.type === 'STOP_RECORDING') {
42:         this._shouldStop = true;
43:         this.port.postMessage({
44:           type: 'DEBUG',
45:           msg: 'STOP_RECORDING flag set',
46:         });
47:       } else if (event.data.type === 'START_RECORDING') {
48:         this._shouldStop = false;
49: 
50:         if (event.data.punchInUserTime_Real !== undefined) {
51:           // FIXED: Use anchorFrame passed from main thread directly
52:           this._anchoredFrame = Math.floor(event.data.anchorFrame);
53:           // FIXED: For "none" pre-roll mode, delay actual capture by headLength
54:           // so the rolling buffer accumulates pre-roll audio during the delay
55:           const shouldDelayCapture = event.data.captureImmediately !== true;
56:           const delayFrames = shouldDelayCapture ? Math.floor(this._headLength * sampleRate) : 0;
57:           const captureStartFrame = currentFrame + delayFrames;
58: 
59:           this._isRecording = true;
60:           this._audioData = [];
61:           this._recordingStartFrame = captureStartFrame;
62: 
63:           this.port.postMessage({
64:             type: 'RECORDING_STARTED',
65:             startTime: captureStartFrame / sampleRate,
66:             anchoredFrame: this._anchoredFrame,
67:             currentTime: currentTime,
68:             delayFrames: delayFrames,
69:             msg: 'Recording: anchorFrame=' + this._anchoredFrame + ', captureStartFrame=' + captureStartFrame + ', delayFrames=' + delayFrames,
70:           });
71:         }
72:       }
73:     };
74:   }
75: 
76:   process(inputs, outputs, parameters) {
77:     this._processCount++;
78: 
79:     // Handle stop flag from messages
80:     if (this._shouldStop) {
81:       this._shouldStop = false;
82:       if (this._isRecording) {
83:         this._isRecording = false;
84:         const flushed = this._flush();
85:         // Send recorded data back to main thread
86:         this.port.postMessage({
87:           type: 'RECORDING_STOPPED',
88:           audioData: flushed[0],
89:           anchoredFrame: flushed[1],
90:           rollingOffset: flushed[2],
91:         });
92:       }
93:     }
94: 
95:     // Always capture to rolling buffer (when playing)
96:     if (inputs && inputs[0] && inputs[0][0]) {
97:       const channelData = inputs[0][0]; // First channel (mono)
98:       const copy = new Float32Array(channelData.length);
99:       copy.set(channelData);
100: 
101:       // Add to rolling buffer
102:       this._rollingBuffer.push({
103:         data: copy,
104:         frame: currentFrame,
105:       });
106: 
107:       // Trim old data (keep rollingBufferDuration seconds)
108:       const maxFrames = this._rollingBufferDuration * sampleRate;
109:       while (this._rollingBuffer.length > 0) {
110:         if (currentFrame - this._rollingBuffer[0].frame > maxFrames) {
111:           this._rollingBuffer.shift();
112:         } else {
113:           break;
114:         }
115:       }
116:     }
117: 
118:     // FIXED: If recording but current frame is before capture start, don't store audio
119:     // This allows the rolling buffer to accumulate pre-roll audio during the delay
120:     const isCapturing = this._isRecording && currentFrame >= this._recordingStartFrame;
121:     if (isCapturing && inputs && inputs[0] && inputs[0][0]) {
122:       const channelData = inputs[0][0];
123:       const copy = new Float32Array(channelData.length);
124:       copy.set(channelData);
125:       this._audioData.push({ data: copy, frame: currentFrame });
126:     }
127: 
128:     return true;
129:   }
130: 
131:   _flush() {
132:     // Combine the rolling buffer (pre-roll) with the recorded data
133:     // The rolling buffer provides the "head" audio before the punch-in point
134:     const allChunks = [];
135:     let rollingOffset = 0;
136: 
137:     if (this._rollingBuffer.length > 0) {
138:       // Find the frame where _recordingStartFrame begins in the rolling buffer
139:       const recordStartFrame = this._recordingStartFrame;
140: 
141:       // Include rolling buffer data up to recording start
142:       for (let i = 0; i < this._rollingBuffer.length; i++) {
143:         const chunk = this._rollingBuffer[i];
144:         const chunkEndFrame = chunk.frame + chunk.data.length;
145: 
146:         if (chunkEndFrame <= recordStartFrame) {
147:           // Entirely before recording start — include as head
148:           allChunks.push(chunk.data);
149:           rollingOffset += chunk.data.length;
150:         } else if (chunk.frame < recordStartFrame) {
151:           // Partially overlaps — only include the pre-recording portion
152:           const offset = recordStartFrame - chunk.frame;
153:           if (offset > 0 && offset < chunk.data.length) {
154:             allChunks.push(chunk.data.subarray(0, offset));
155:             rollingOffset += offset;
156:           }
157:           break;
158:         } else {
159:           break;
160:         }
161:       }
162:     }
163: 
164:     // Append the recorded audio data
165:     if (this._audioData.length > 0) {
166:       const totalRecordingLength = this._audioData.reduce((sum, chunk) => sum + chunk.data.length, 0);
167:       const combinedRecording = new Float32Array(totalRecordingLength);
168:       let offset = 0;
169:       this._audioData.forEach(chunk => {
170:         combinedRecording.set(chunk.data, offset);
171:         offset += chunk.data.length;
172:       });
173:       allChunks.push(combinedRecording);
174:     }
175: 
176:     // Combine everything into one array
177:     let totalLength = 0;
178:     allChunks.forEach(c => totalLength += c.length);
179:     const result = new Float32Array(totalLength);
180:     let offset = 0;
181:     allChunks.forEach(c => { result.set(c, offset); offset += c.length; });
182: 
183:     // FIXED: Return _anchoredFrame (the sync anchor from main thread) instead of _recordingStartFrame
184:     // _anchoredFrame represents the timeline-relative frame number for the punch-in point
185:     const returnAnchorFrame = this._anchoredFrame;
186:     this.port.postMessage({
187:       type: 'DEBUG',
188:       msg: '_flush() returning',
189:       recordingStartFrame: this._recordingStartFrame,
190:       anchoredFrame: returnAnchorFrame,
191:       rollingOffset: rollingOffset,
192:       audioDataLength: result.length,
193:       sampleRate: sampleRate,
194:     });
195:     this._audioData = [];
196:     return [result, returnAnchorFrame, rollingOffset];
197:   }
198: }
```

All three core fixes have been applied and verified:

1. **Worklet `_flush()` fix**: Now returns `_anchoredFrame` (the timeline-relative sync anchor from main thread) instead of `_recordingStartFrame` (physical capture start). Also adds delayed capture for "none" pre-roll mode so the rolling buffer accumulates pre-roll audio.

3. **useAudioEngine fix**: Flattens audio data (url, audioBuffer, peaks) to the top level of trackOptions so that `multitrack.initAudio()` can find them at `track.audioBuffer`/`track.url` instead of nested inside `track.phrases[]`.

## Assistant (Build · Ring 2.6 1T Free · 23.9s)

_Thinking:_

The user hasn't asked a new question - the system just sent a format reminder. I've already completed all the work and the results are clear:

1. All 3 files were fixed
